headline: Aircraft deliveries hit record high as global demand fuels £388bn UK aerospace pipeline
why: Record deliveries and a £388bn UK aerospace pipeline capture the Supplier side of the cascade at full throttle — the immediate cash-on-delivery benefit of years of airline capex — while the sheer backlog size raises the question of whether this is approaching Peak.
data_to_pull: UK aerospace order intake vs deliveries ratio
Airbus and Boeing delivery counts YoY

headline: UK defence plan leaves US$6.5 billion hole in Burnham's first Budget
why: UK defence capex is inflecting up hard, and the $6.5B budget gap quantifies the fiscal strain of the build — a classic boom-phase signal where the Investor's commitment is outrunning available capital.
data_to_pull: UK defence spend as % GDP trajectory
Major UK defence procurement line items
BAE Systems backlog and order intake

headline: SAS is said to near major Airbus widebody order in fleet expansion push
why: Clean Investor→Supplier linkage: SAS (airline, Investor) ordering Airbus widebodies (Supplier books backlog and revenue now), with the bust risk landing years later on SAS if widebody capacity oversupplies routes.
data_to_pull: SAS fleet age and replacement schedule
Airbus widebody order backlog and delivery slots

headline: What's in Mayor TenHaken's final, $1.1B five-year capital spending plan
why: Concrete $1.1B municipal capex figure over five years — modest in scale but a trackable investor-side build number with supplier downstream effects.
